summary:
I'll skip task tracking since this is a focused single-pass build that's nearly complete. Just need the `site_intent.json` and a verification pass.
All files are in place. Quick summary of the build:

**Built the National Curling Federation portal** — a dense, utilitarian institutional site in icy blue (#BCD7E6), granite (#3A3F45), and white, with a workhorse-grotesk + mono-tabular type system.

**Files written (11):**
- `index.html` — masthead, scrolling results ticker, three-column dashboard (rankings tabs / live playoff bracket teaser / news + officiating notice), club-of-the-week spotlight with topographic ice motif, sponsor strip, full footer
- `standings.html` — chip-rail filters, three division tabs, sortable 12-team table with inline SVG sparklines, legend & scoring sidebar, CSV/PDF actions
- `athletes.html` — featured national-squad block, left filter rail (discipline/position/region/hand/age), 18 athlete cards with silhouette portraits & position chips, pagination
- `brackets.html` — tournament header, live final banner, RR/playoff toggle, full 4-round bracket with end-by-end score grids and hammer indicators, draw-schedule table, assigned-officials panel
- `clubs.html` — chip-rail filters, two-pane locator (inline SVG national map with 5 labelled regions + 15 club markers shaded by sheet count, scrollable list pane), click-through flyouts
- `rules.html` — sticky chapter TOC, handbook with §-numbered sections, inline SVG diagrams (ice sheet / granite stone / free-guard zone), rule-change callouts, 5-rung officiating certification ladder, subscribe form
- `results.html` — chip filters, season range slider 1976→2026, grouped results tables with gold/silver/bronze medals and expandable end-by-end detail rows, historical-champions carousel, year facet sidebar
- `news.html` — chip filters, news hero, 10-bulletin two-column list with category tags, officiating notices sidebar with subscribe form, athlete-of-the-month card
- `styles.css` — shared design system (palette, ticker animation, tables, chips, bracket, map overlay, handbook, etc.)
- `app.js` — vanilla JS for tabs, chip toggles, sortable tables, expandable result rows, bracket view toggle, map↔list linking, subscribe-form confirmation
- `site_intent.json` — concept-as-implemented manifest
